MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR AWARD OF DEFENSE COSTS, INCLUDING ATTORNEY'S FEES, C.C.P. SECTION 1038; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES, GOVT. CODE SECTION 12965(b).

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-10-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: David Lipps of Hoyer & Associates (956.1360) for plaintiff and responding party; Nancy A. Huneke of Stubbs & Leone (925.974.8600) for defendant and moving party. Argued and the court ruled as follows: Plaintiff's oral request for a continuance is denied. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant met its summary judgment burden by showing that it had a legitimate reason for not interviewing plaintiff. Plaintiff failed to present sufficient evidence to prove that defendant's decision was motivated by race, gender, plaintiff's disability, or a retaliatory motive. Further plaintiff's claims for disability discrimination and retaliation are untimely and plaintiff failed to exhaust his administrative remedies. The only FEHA charges that reference those issues were filed in April 2008 and the right to sue letter on those claims were issued on May 1, 2008. Plaintiff filed this action more than a year later. The right to sue letter issued November 25, 2008 was based on a charge that only referenced racial and sexual discrimination. Under Okoli v. Lockheed Technical Operations Co. (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 1607. Plaintiff failed to exhaust his administrative remedies. Order re: motion and order re: objections signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF PETER HEIN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES.

APPEARANCES: David Lipps, Esq. of Hoyer & Associates 926-1360 for pltf & moving party; Marina B. Pitts, Esq. of Law Offices Stubbs & Leone 925/974-8600 for deft & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANT IN PART AND DENY IN PART. DENY AS TO PRODUCTION NUMBER 3 BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS RESPONDED. DENY AS TO PRODUCTION 11 AS OVERBROAD AND PLAINTIFF DOES NOT MEET THE HARDING LAWSON STANDARD. GRANT AS TO PRODUCTION 12 AND 13. DEFENDANT SHALL TURN OVER DOCUMENTS REGARDING THE DECISION TO HIRE DUBON, HOWEVER DEFENDANT SHALL NOT TURN OVER HER PERSONNEL FILE. GRANT AS TO INTERROGATORIES 12.6, 16.10, AND 204.3-204.7, INCLUSIVE. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED, DEFENDANT HAD SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR ITS DECISION. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Marina B. Pitts, Esq., for defendant and moving party: San Francisco Community College District
DAvid Lipps, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Peter Hein

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICt's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Written Discovery is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall provide defendant with further responses to interrogatories and documents that are properly categorized and stamped with bates labels sufficient to identify the document and the request that the document is responsive to within 10 days from the hearing date. Requests for Sanctions are denied. Judge: MONICA A. WILEY, not reported.
